steps.title: Set up
steps.content: In right triangle ABC with the right angle at B, the angle at C is 57°. Side AB is opposite the 57° angle, and AC is the hypotenuse. Use the sine ratio from angle C.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
$$\sin(57°) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{AB}{AC}$$
Solving for AB:
$$AB = AC \cdot \sin(57°)$$
With AC as the reference unit, $AB = \sin(57°)$.
